# terrible_f5networks_f5_modules_bigip_iapp_service (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) The name of the iApp service you want to deploy.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the iApp service. If this option is specified in the Ansible task, it takes precedence over any similar setting in the iApp Service payload that you provide in the `parameters` field.
- `device_group` (String) The device group for the iApp service. If this option is specified in the Ansible task, it takes precedence over any similar setting in the iApp Service payload that you provide in the `parameters` field.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `force` (Boolean) Forces the updating of an iApp service, even if the parameters to the service have not changed. This option is of particular importance if the iApp template that underlies the service has been updated in-place. This option is equivalent to re-configuring the iApp if that template has changed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `metadata` (String) Metadata associated with the iApp service. If this option is specified in the Ansible task, it takes precedence over any similar setting in the iApp Service payload that you provide in the `parameters` field.
- `parameters` (String) A hash of all the required template variables for the iApp template. If your parameters are stored in a file (the more common scenario) we recommend you use either the `file` or `template` lookups to supply the expected parameters. These parameters typically consist of the `lists`, `tables`, and `variables` fields.
- `partition` (String) Device partition to manage resources on.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) When `present`, ensures the iApp service is created and running. When `absent`, ensures the iApp service has been removed.
- `strict_updates` (Boolean) Indicates whether the application service is tied to the template, so when the template is updated, the application service changes to reflect the updates. When `true`, disallows any updates to the resources that the iApp service has created, if they are not updated directly through the iApp. When `false`, allows updates outside of the iApp. If this option is specified in the Ansible task, it takes precedence over any similar setting in the iApp Service payload that you provide in the `parameters` field.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `template` (String) The iApp template from which to instantiate a new service. This template must exist on your BIG-IP before you can successfully create a service. When creating a new service, this parameter is required.
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `traffic_group` (String) The traffic group for the iApp service. When creating a new service, if this value is not specified, the default of `/Common/traffic-group-1` is used. If this option is specified in the Ansible task, it takes precedence over any similar setting in the iApp Service payload that you provide in the `parameters` field.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
